frontier_summary: W1452 is a nonterminal but useful frontier shift: it assembles W987 from two raw W716 boundary fields, two raw W717 relation fields, carried W984 target/closedness data, and carried W950 normalized-source/endpoint data. W716 and W717 are already split to raw fields; the remaining bundled assumptions at the W1452 surface are W984 and W950, with product success still false.

route_decision.recommended_surface: W984 internals: replace the carried TargetNoUnivClosedEmbeddingClosednessDataProviderW984 input in the W1452-style construction surface with its W944 target-no-univ and W973 closed-embedding closedness components.
route_decision.why_this_reduces_frontier: W1452 has already split the W716 and W717 inputs, while W1450/W1452 still carry W984 as one bundled provider. W1440 proves the W984 component unpacks to W944 and W973, so a direct W1453-style construction that carries raw W716/W717 plus W944/W973 plus W950 would remove one of the two remaining bundled provider assumptions without replaying wrapper or rewrap machinery.
route_decision.why_not_wrapper_cycle: W1440-W1449 unpack, package, rewrap, and stable-output routes are documented as nonterminal or rejected cycles. W1450-W1452 moved to field-data construction; the next increment should continue discharging carried fields rather than rebuilding W987/W1007 or repeating provider packaging.

rejected_routes.reason: W1451 and W1452 already split W716 and W717 into their two raw fields and prove projections back to those fields; targeting the same split again would duplicate LCA-SUP-009/W1451-W1452 rather than reduce the next bundled assumption.

Viable but second choice: W1441 provides the W950 split into W948/W949, but W984 is earlier in the W1450/W1452 carried-provider pair and has a simple two-field W1440 target/closedness unpack already recorded.

implementation_note: This is an audit-only route decision. Do not edit the target repo from this worker result alone; parent/governor should verify and, if accepted, add an implementation note before the Lean implementation role consumes it.
